headings: h2: Online Safety Act Campaign – Survey Results
text_excerpt: Online Safety Act Campaign – Survey Results – Emily Darlington MP Emily Darlington MP Championing a Fairer, Greener, and Brighter Future for Milton Keynes Central About Emily How Is Emily Working for you? Weekly Newsletter News & Views Online Safety Act Campaign – Survey Results Useful Resources Contact Emily Surgery Appointments Data Protection and Privacy Policy Online Safety Act Campaign – Survey Results As part of my ongoing online safety campaign, I carried out a survey of 14-16 year olds in schools across Milton Keynes Central to hear directly from young people about their experiences online. It explores how social media and digital platforms affect them, what they feel works (and doesn’t) when it comes to online safety, and what changes they believe the government should consider. The survey also examined young people’s views on potential restrictions or bans, to understand the level of support and concern around these ideas. Below, I’ve linked the survey results, which reflect the voices, experiences, and opinions shared by participants. If you have any questions about this campaign or would like to discuss the survey findings, please contact my office. Stay in the loop!
